content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 30, 1990

Bradley W. Hertz  
Reed & Davidson  
888 West Sixth Street  
12th Floor  
Los Angeles,, CA 90017

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-597

Dear Mr. Hertz:

We have received your letter dated September 10, 1990, seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice provided by this agency on September 5 and 6, 1990 regarding Section 85600 of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1

Except as modified below, your letter accurately summarizes the advice provided by this agency. In an independent expenditure mass mailing which contains a letter, brochure, two response devices and a bumper sticker, the following placement of the "Notice to Voters" Statement complies with Section 85600.

The envelope displays the disclaimer.

One side of a one-page, two-sided letter displays the disclaimer.

A 8-1/2" x 11" brochure folded into thirds contains three disclaimers, one on each fold.

So long as they are enclosed with other items displaying the disclaimer, the two response devices and bumper sticker need not contain disclaimers.

The disclaimer is the one set forth in Section 85600(a), is in at least 10-point type, is not subjected to the half-tone or screening process, and is in a printed or drawn box set apart from any other printed matter. (Section 85600(b).)

In addition, the envelope and at least one insert should display the name, street address, and city of the sending

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

committee. The information should be in no less than six-point type in a color or print which contrast with the background so as to be easily legible. (Section 84305.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: [signature]
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MTM:dg
